model_name_policy: Every recommended_models[].model value must not be normalized before passing to --model.
selection_rules: Use the first recommended model unless cost, latency, privacy, or provider availability pushes you to another listed model.
Use High, Thinking, fable, opus, or gpt-5.5 for ambiguous, high-risk, multi-file, long-context, security, or architecture work.
Use Low, mini, haiku, or GPT-OSS 120B (Medium) for bounded, repetitive, low-risk, or private/offline work.
Use provider diversity for review: ask a different provider than the one that produced the first draft.

codex.gpt-5.5.role: OpenAI flagship for complex reasoning, coding, tools, and long-context professional work.

codex.gpt-5.4.role: Strong general coding and professional-work model with good cost/performance.

codex.gpt-5.4-mini.role: Fast, lower-cost option for simpler coding, test, doc, and subagent-style tasks.

antigravity.gemini-3.5-flash-medium.role: Default balanced Gemini option for agentic coding, iterative implementation, and most tasks.

antigravity.gemini-3.5-flash-high.role: Higher-reasoning Gemini option for hard coding, planning, tool use, and debugging.

antigravity.gemini-3.5-flash-low.role: Fast Gemini option for simple analysis, writing, and low-risk code loops.

antigravity.gemini-3.1-pro-high.role: Deep-reasoning, multimodal, long-context option for broad datasets and complex tradeoffs.

antigravity.claude-sonnet-4.6-thinking.role: Balanced high-quality Claude option for coding, agent planning, knowledge work, and design.

antigravity.claude-opus-4.6-thinking.role: Careful, long-context Claude option for hard planning, debugging, code review, and large codebases.

antigravity.gpt-oss-120b-medium.role: Open-weight high-reasoning option for private, local, offline, or vendor-diverse work.

claude-code.fable.role: Top Claude Code alias for long-running agentic work, hard planning, and expert review.

claude-code.opus.role: Claude Code alias for complex agentic coding, large refactors, root-cause analysis, and review.

claude-code.sonnet.role: Default Claude Code alias for everyday coding, planning, review, docs, and tests.

claude-code.haiku.role: Fast Claude Code alias for simple, bounded, low-risk writing and coding tasks.

discovery_research.agile_context: Product goal discovery, domain research, user/problem exploration, market and technical feasibility.

recommended_models.why: Best when the research may turn into long-running agentic planning.
Strong for complex synthesis and professional reasoning.
Good for broad-context, multimodal, and dataset-heavy research.
Careful long-context research and expert-grade synthesis.
Balanced research when latency matters.

requirements_user_stories.agile_context: Write user stories, acceptance criteria, edge cases, constraints, and definition of done.

recommended_models.why: Best default for clear, practical story/spec writing.
Strong instruction following, knowledge work, and design detail.
Solid professional writing plus coding awareness.
Fast for straightforward story cleanup.
Cheap fast option for low-risk formatting and story splitting.

backlog_refinement_prioritization.agile_context: Split backlog items, refine scope, estimate, find dependencies, rank by value and risk.

recommended_models.why: Default for practical backlog cleanup and task splitting.
Good for product, design, and implementation tradeoffs.
Good agentic planner for iterative backlog work.
Good cost/performance for everyday engineering planning.
Fast option for small, obvious refinement.

sprint_planning.agile_context: Create sprint goals, implementation plans, task breakdowns, dependency checks, and risk notes.

recommended_models.why: Best OpenAI option for complex reasoning and coding plans.
Careful planning for larger codebases and long-horizon tasks.
Use when the plan may need long-running agentic execution.
High-effort Gemini path for hard code and agent plans.
Default for normal sprint/task plans.

architecture_design.agile_context: System design, architecture tradeoffs, data model/API design, migration strategy, non-functional requirements.

recommended_models.why: Strongest option for high-stakes long-horizon architecture.
Strong complex reasoning and coding-aware design.
Careful design review for large codebases and edge cases.
Good for broad multimodal/long-context tradeoff analysis.
Good Claude Code option for implementation-oriented architecture.

ui_ux_prototyping.agile_context: UX flows, frontend prototypes, design-system-aware UI implementation, accessibility and interaction design.

recommended_models.why: Strong balance of frontend code, design sense, and knowledge work.
Strong for frontend coding and computer-use workflows.
Use for complex frontend architecture or production-grade flows.
Good for coding agents and interactive prototyping.
Good default for everyday frontend work.

implementation_coding.agile_context: Implement backlog items, refactor small areas, wire APIs, edit code, and run local verification.

recommended_models.why: Best default for everyday implementation.
Strong coding model with good cost/performance.
Good for iterative coding loops and agentic execution.
Use for higher-quality implementation and design-sensitive code.
Fast for small, bounded changes.

debugging_root_cause.agile_context: Reproduce bugs, trace callers, find root cause, patch shared logic, and verify regressions.

recommended_models.why: Strong at careful debugging, code review, and large codebases.
Good Claude Code path for root-cause work.
Strong for hard reasoning and coding fixes.
Good for hard coding/debugging with tools.
Default for normal bugfixes.

code_review_quality.agile_context: Review diffs, find regressions, check maintainability, identify missing tests, and validate acceptance criteria.

recommended_models.why: Strong code review and mistake-catching on larger codebases.
Use for high-stakes review and cross-repo context.
Good independent frontier review.
Good balanced review for normal PRs.
Good provider-diverse review for hard diffs.

testing_qa.agile_context: Test strategy, unit/integration/e2e test generation, acceptance checks, QA scenarios, and regression verification.

recommended_models.why: Strong for coding tests and verification logic.
Good default for writing focused tests.
Good for iterative test generation and tool loops.
Useful private/offline high-reasoning test work.
Fast for simple unit tests and expected-output checks.

security_review.agile_context: Threat modeling, secure design review, vulnerability triage, auth/data boundary checks, and abuse-case review.

recommended_models.why: Strong for cybersecurity-oriented review and deep reasoning.
Use for high-stakes security analysis.
Strong independent security reasoning.
Good for broad system and document context.
Use when code or findings must stay local/private.

release_deployment.agile_context: Release plan, deployment checklist, CI/CD notes, rollback plan, migration steps, and release notes.

recommended_models.why: Good for practical deployment and automation tasks.
Good default for checklists and release engineering.
Good for multi-step tool and deployment planning.
Good for careful runbooks and release notes.
Fast for simple release-note drafting.

workspace_setup.agile_context: Set up or detect isolated workspaces, worktrees, repo baseline state, local environment, and pre-implementation verification.

recommended_models.why: Fast for bounded workspace and repository setup checks.
Cheap option for simple setup and verification commands.
Default when setup needs platform judgment or recovery.
Use when setup touches several project conventions.
Good for multi-step setup in agentic tool loops.

monitoring_incident_ops.agile_context: Monitoring design, incident triage, log synthesis, postmortems, service reliability, and operational follow-up.

recommended_models.why: Good for long-running operational investigation.
Strong at root-cause analysis and long-context reasoning.
Strong for incident synthesis and remediation plans.
Good for hard multi-step ops reasoning.
Good for large docs/log context and multimodal evidence.

documentation_knowledge_transfer.agile_context: Developer docs, ADRs, changelogs, onboarding docs, handoff notes, and knowledge-base cleanup.

recommended_models.why: Good default for clear engineering documentation.
Strong for polished knowledge work.
Good professional writing with code awareness.
Fast for straightforward docs and summaries.
Fast for low-risk formatting, summaries, and changelogs.

retrospective_process_improvement.agile_context: Retrospective facilitation, process metrics, working-agreement updates, and improvement experiments.

recommended_models.why: Strong for cross-signal reasoning and improvement planning.
Good for long-horizon process and org reasoning.
Good for synthesizing broad qualitative and quantitative inputs.
Good for practical knowledge-work outputs.
Default for normal retrospective notes and action items.
